meta.title: Institutional-Grade Staking in ETFs With Helius and Bitwise
meta.product: Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL), Helius Staking

transcript:
Alexandra Nadel: Hi everyone. Thank you for joining us today. My name is Ali Nadel and I'm an Associate Director of Business Development on the Onchain Solutions team at Bitwise Asset Management. And with me today we have Hong Kim, CTO and co-founder of Bitwise Asset Management, along with Mert Mumtaz, who is the CEO and co-founder of Helius. Thanks for joining us, Hong and Mert.

So this audience is likely quite interested in learning about how staking has entered into ETFs. So let's start by diving right in and learning about the partnership between Bitwise and Helius on uh, staking for the product that we launched back in October called BSOL, which is the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF, uh, trading or listed on the New York Stock Exchange, trading under the ticker BSOL. So Hong, if we could start with you and learn a little bit about how you and Mert put your heads together to form the staking partnership, that would be wonderful.

Hong Kim: Amazing. Yeah. Uh, great to be here. Um, and yeah. When when the the US ETF launch moment is a pretty important moment because uh, US is ultimately the largest capital market in the traditional financial world. And also the spot ETFs ends up becoming the most liquid product as we've seen from IBIT and ETHA etc. where um, so it it has a pretty important role that it plays and once it grows and gains liquidity then it becomes this huge thing. Um, and so we wanted to be thoughtful about how we engage with staking. Uh, and staking isn't just a little bit different from custody, it isn't just something that sits statically, it engages with the network in a meaningful way. So how a large amount of stake that is tied to a product engages with the network can have a lot of uh, influence and distortions at times. So uh, we were looking for a partner that can help us navigate that. Um, and there are a lot of uh, partner staking providers that can say that they have good performance and uptime and all these things, but staking in a proof of stake network is not just about that. Uh, it's about the small decisions that that think about whether the network becomes more useful and easier for app developers. It is about um, decentralization as well. And so uh, no better partner than Helius uh and Mert here to help us really understand and navigate all of those decisions. And so that that was uh, maybe maybe that kind of there's the obvious things like Helius is the largest validator and uh been doing it and great performance and all these things, but maybe the kind of less obvious thing is is that it was important to us to have a partner that we can feel uh can can guide us there. Yeah.

Alexandra Nadel: Yeah. And Mert, would love to hear from you on what's unique about Helius's staking and what does the setup look like for BSOL specifically?

Mert Mumtaz: Sure. Um, in terms of what's unique, uh, well, we are one of the only teams, I mean there's there's quite a few, I don't want to I don't want to like uh, you know, gas too much, but um, I I would I would say first of all we're extremely network aligned. And what what I mean by that is it's very easy to make certain short-term decisions to let's say boost yield by like, you know, let's say 0.5% but increasing the risk of let's say being front run or or or the network going down by like 20% or something. But um, since we actually have another business and our core business is not validator, it's actually uh running infrastructure of which validators are a single part of, we also run by far the most number of nodes on the network, right? We also run RPC nodes, uh, you know, thousands of RPC nodes, which are actually just validator staking nodes except without um, voting. Which which means we've seen basically every possible variation of anything that can go wrong um, on all different regions in the world. And so when you have institutional money and and all eyes are on you, you can actually feel safe that, okay, these guys have done this before, they have done it for three years, um, uh, and and they've been battle tested, right? Like we've been, not us, but like Solana itself has been attacked, uh, you know, blockchains are relatively adversarial networks, and we've been kind of there on the front lines, you know, navigating, okay, how do we get around this, etc. Um, and and and also like the other thing is uh the the stakers themselves, the holders of BSOL get really the best possible economics because we don't need to make money ourselves on the stake from Bitwise's validator, right? Um, our business is running is helping developers, right? It's making the network faster so that people can actually build the future of internet capital markets, right? Perps, DEXes, uh spot trading, borrow lend, uh uh in in in the fastest possible way on decentralized rails. And then every decision that we make is is is is tailored to help towards that because our goal is to just get people to build on Solana. And so this is like a really interesting um, partnership where like you can literally buy a stock that stakes with Bitwise's validator run by us, but then since we use that to make the network faster, buying the thing can literally help developers build faster applications.

Hong Kim: I think that's also that's a really important point uh from my perspective. Like uh, compared to let's say like a network on Ethereum, um, Solana the the applications really take advantage of the extra like last 1% of performance to compete and and uh uh deliver the the the next kind of marginal amount of more liquidity or marginal amount of of of scale to their users. So whether the nodes are actually supporting that to the maximum extent does matter. And uh so so so the running of the node is not as kind of like you just download the thing, you run it and there's really not much difference between whether this stake uh this validator has more stake or that validator has more stake. The impact on the network, the users, the applications that can be built uh uh there's a lot of uh networks where that really the relationship doesn't matter as much. But on Solana the the the application landscape is changing all the time, the the protocol spec is improving, the the the scale is imp- so so it really does um have a have a big impact. And that is uh a big big part of it for us because ultimately the we want the ecosystem, the developers, the users of Solana, the whole community to feel that the the increasing stake weight uh stake weight that comes from this product, this regulated uh product existing isn't something that deteriorates uh the market structure or the ecosystem, but rather uh plays a role in making it more stable and making it more supportive. And yeah, so that that's that's an that that's a thing that's really important for us.

Alexandra Nadel: Great. So before BSOL launched in the US, we had staking and then we had ETFs. But since the launch of BSOL, we now have staking included in ETFs. So curious if you guys can share a little bit about the challenges you faced in getting staking approved and and working properly in a regulated ETF product.

Hong Kim: What did your team complain the most about?

Mert Mumtaz: Well uh, we did we did all the hard work of waiting for uh the Bitwise team to figure it out. Uh, we we just um, our job is really the technical side of things, right? So um, you know, ETFs will have specific reporting requirements so we need to get quite high quality data, uh check the accuracy, make sure that um, you know, the the the um, the granularity of the data, but also Solana is kind of hard to read, make sense of, right? And and for an institution product like this if the data is, you know, if there's even the slightest kind of error in in the little detail, that ends up kind of maybe missing investors. But since we're a data infrastructure company we really take that seriously so but but I would say that was um, that's something we take super seriously and so that was a unique challenge for us because most of our customers aren't, you know, the the first staking ETF for Solana. Um, but I'm I'm sure you guys had a much different story there.

Hong Kim: No, I I I think reporting is definitely one of the one because if you think about an ETF it has a daily uh reconciliation and uh striking of NAV and such. So the 4:00 PM Eastern to the 4:00 PM Eastern has to be exact. And what was exactly the staking rewards that were accumulated in that time. And then uh it's not just we we have a view, we need to send that to our fund administrators, the custodian, and our auditors all have to reconcile and audit that on a on on a daily schedule. And so those are the the the kind of additional requirements of uh a regulated product that in some ways is that amount of oversight and like multi-party reconciliation on a daily basis um is is at times onerous and onerous about um um making a product like this work. But at the same time it it really uh also a thing that drives a lot of confidence in these products as well. Like you can know when the NAV moves up this much uh on a daily basis like that is actually what happened. And um so yeah that that's definitely one of the elements. And and then after that it was really like working with the SEC on on getting them comfortable uh with with how Solana staking works um and stuff like that. Yeah.

Alexandra Nadel: Great. So Hong, as the audience likely has, you know, institutional investors, how does Bitwise go about explaining how Solana staking works to institutions?

Hong Kim: Yeah, that's a good question. Um, I think the uh enough people have heard about Proof of Stake that they understand what roughly it is or the kind of the the role that that that it plays. I think um actually explaining uh the product to to like large banks and platforms, the main thing that we have to deal with is is how uh Solana staking is different from for example like Ethereum or so where a lot of the questions are around like illiquidity and whether the the exit queue is going to be a month and does this mean that this product is is going to have a lot of uh gotchas for investors because once you stake the the the assets are tied there. And we tell them uh each epoch is two days and uh it it doesn't really work like that. And then also there's a lot of uh concern around slashing and then like loss of investor funds as a result of that and then we walk them through how how that's not the case in Solana. So it was more of those types of conversations that I think we're uh having, I've been having those conversations maybe like twice a week since we launched the product with various institutions just help them understand. So but that's the role that Bitwise plays is that like it might be obvious to the people that are in this room and and living and breathing it on a daily basis, but for the people that are just hearing it for the first time, they have no idea. So working with their priors, wherever they are, uh trying to get them closer to what the actual reality is is a big part of yeah yeah what we do.

Alexandra Nadel: Great. Uh, well we're getting close on time here so one final question for you both. Now that we have Solana staking ETFs, what is the next milestone for Solana?

Hong Kim: In the kind of like capital markets track? Yeah.

Mert Mumtaz: Or so you mean for Solana as an ecosystem in general?

Alexandra Nadel: Yeah.

Mert Mumtaz: Yeah. Um, oof. Well, I I would say really the job's not going to be finished until you can trade any market at any time from your pocket on the internet with nothing but an internet connection. Right? Um, we we just uh for for for folks who went to the other stage, Ellipsis just announced a perps DEX, right? This is like one of the areas where Solana was kind of behind in. Kalshi announced that they're tokenizing uh prediction markets on Solana. And we also already have great this new initiative of listing new assets on Solana super fast. So for example Monad was a day one listing on Solana. So you take, you know, spot listings, perps, prediction markets, you know, the long tail of assets, but then also institutional things, right? So like tokenized uh stocks uh uh with Superstate or X-Stocks, which you know they're kind of different products but the idea is that they're they're um stocks. You you you add things like, you know, you can redeem natural gold on Solana uh and even uranium soon, right? So you take all these different things and you basically what you have is like the internet's market. You have the Amazon of markets and you can trade anything you want with just open up your phone uh and and having an internet connection. Um, not financial advice. And uh 24/7 around the world. And that's really the endgame, right? And then the devil is just in the details of okay what are the changes that you need to make to enable this, right? Like how do you actually remove friction for new folks? How do you make sure that, you know, the market structure is such that it's not extractive to retail, right? Like how do you make sure they don't get front run? Um, and you know you can we have all these different boring nerdy things like multiple concurrent uh proposers, but fundamentally it comes down to the four letters that we always say which is IBRL: Increase Bandwidth Reduce Latency, right? Once you make the network as fast as possible, like a giant basically science fiction finance computer, and you have all the market's liquidity concentrated in one place on the internet, I think that really allows for basically the evolution of capitalism in a sense.

Alexandra Nadel: Great. Anything quickly add there Hong?

Hong Kim: Yeah. I would I would reinforce that. Maybe the perspective that I have is a little bit more of like TradFi uh and and BSOL being now like fully basically Solana is uh this kind of Solana's IPO moment. Like you can trade Solana on the New York Stock Exchange now and and also in a staked wrapper. Um, and uh excited about the tokenization part that that Mert talked about because that enables then it to travel the other direction. And then now you can have lending markets on literally the same same ticker same asset where you can maybe get uh a a loan on your staked Solana position at Goldman Sachs at your prime or on uh uh a a lending protocol on Solana in uh in DeFi. So those two worlds merging on literally the same uh type of instrument is is I think the liquidity is coming together and really the next stage of Solana where it's not an isolated market but is actually uh just integrated into the whole.

Alexandra Nadel: Excellent. Well this has been such a great talk. Thank you all for joining us. If anyone has any questions, everyone should have a a card on their seats with uh my Telegram information so feel free to reach out, ask any questions. Uh and let's give it up for both Hong and Mert. Thank you.

Hong Kim: Thank you.

sources.title: Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
sources.snippet:
Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in AUM
# Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
San Francisco •Nov 21, 2025
![Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.datocms-assets.com%2F62087%2F1762788442-bsol-bitwise-solana-staking-etf-press-500m-aum.png&w=3840&q=75)
Bitwise Asset Management, the global crypto asset manager with over $15 billion in client assets, announced today that the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) has surpassed $500 million in assets under management (AUM), achieving the landmark figure in its first 18 days of trading.
"We are truly in awe of the speed and scale of BSOL’s growth," said Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley. "Crypto is becoming a mainstream asset class. BSOL hitting half a billion dollars in AUM this quickly is a clear testament to investors’ belief in the Solana ecosystem as a cornerstone of the future of the crypto industry."
As one of the first crypto ETPs with built-in staking, BSOL aims to stake 100% of the Fund’s SOL holdings through Bitwise Onchain Solutions, powered by Helius, the leading Solana staking technology known for its high performance, technical excellence, and security. The strategy seeks to maximize investors’ participation in Solana’s average staking rewards of over 7%.¹
BSOL has a net sponsor fee of 0.00% for the first three months on its first $1 billion in assets. All staking fees are also waived for the first three months. After three months, the sponsor fee will be 0.20% and the staking fee, which applies only to the staking rewards that BSOL earns, will be 0.06%.
###### **Note:**
###### (1) Staking reward rate is based on the annualized 90-day average as of 11/20/25, with data from Helius. Rewards are subject to change and should not be considered an indication of the Fund’s performance. The tax treatment of rewards earned by the Trust may have potentially adverse consequences for shareholders. Please consult with your tax advisor.
### Risks and Important Information
**This material must be accompanied by a prospectus. Please read the prospectus carefully before investing. To obtain a current prospectus visit**[**bsoletf.com/welcome**](https://bsoletf.com/welcome)**.**
**The Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) is not suitable for all investors. An investment in BSOL is subject to a high degree of risk, has the potential for significant volatility, and could result in significant or complete loss of investment. BSOL is not an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the “1940 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same protections as ETFs and mutual funds registered under the 1940 Act. An investment in BSOL is not the same as a direct investment in Solana (SOL).**
Shares of ETPs are bought and sold at market price (not NAV) and are not individually redeemed from the Fund. Brokerage commissions will reduce returns. The NAV may not always correspond to the market price of SOL and, as a result, Creation Units may be created or redeemed at a value that is different from the market price of the Shares. Authorized Participants’ buying and selling activity associated with the creation and redemption of Creation Units may adversely affect an investment in the Shares.
The amount of SOL represented by a Share will continue to be reduced during the life of the Fund due to the transfer of the Fund’s SOL to pay for the Sponsor’s management fee, and to pay for litigation expenses or other extraordinary expenses. This dynamic will occur irrespective of whether the trading price of the Shares rises or falls in response to changes in the price of SOL.
There is no guarantee or assurance that the Fund’s methodology will result in the Fund achieving positive investment returns or outperforming other investment products.
Investors may choose to use the Fund as a means of investing

sources.title: Bitwise Asset Management launches the first US spot ETF for Solana
sources.snippet:
Companies 0 [See All](https://www.insidermonkey.com/search/all/?m=2)

[Click here to see all results for](https://www.insidermonkey.com/search/) [Sorry, no results has been found matching your query.](javascript:void(0))

### Share

On October 28, 2025, Bitwise Asset Management launched the BSOL and therefore joined the crypto ETF club.  It’s marketed as the first US ETF to grant spot access to Solana and also offer staking rewards.  This shows how complex the crypto ETF market has become.

The product will likely capture investors’ interest, as it offers many of the features they are looking for.  It’s a way to diversify from Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, and the dual offering of price exposure and staking yield is an interesting innovation.

## What are Crypto ETFs?

Crypto ETFs are exchange-traded funds whose value is tied to crypto.  That way, it gives investors a chance to tie their investments to crypto prices without [using crypto](https://www.webopedia.com/crypto/exchanges/) exchanges or buying crypto coins themselves.

The ETFs have quickly become a staple of investment portfolios, as they provide a good balance to hedge against more risk-averse investments.  New altcoins are joining in quick succession, and it’s widely seen as a way to bridge the gap between crypto and traditional investments.

## The Road to BSOL: New SEC Rules and a Fast-Track Path

Until just recently, the process for getting an ETF spot approved was long and complicated, and it took Bitcoin over a year.  However, in September this year, the SEC approved a generic list of standard cryptos eligible for the spot.

This regulatory shift made things easier for Bitwise, and it’s part of a broader pro-crypto attitude the current administration has taken.  There are even talks of setting up stablecoin ETFs, though these are of lesser value to investors.  Cryptos are already widely adopted, and now that a regulatory landscape has made them safe, the government has taken a pro-business approach.

## How the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF Works

The design centers on two pillars:

**Spot SOL exposure**.  The fund holds all of its assets in Solana tokens.  This means the fund’s value depends on the market price of Solana.  In this regard, the fund is similar to any other crypto ETF.

**Staking reward capture**.  The fund aims to stake 100 percent of its SOL holdings.  That way, holders also receive rewards from staking as if they owned actual Solana coins.  These rewards are then added back to the fund.  It’s the first ETF to do so.

A Blockbuster Debut: Volume, Flows, and Industry Reaction

The launch made somewhat of a splash within the industry.  Millions of dollars in inflows came in as soon as the ETF was listed, and strong trading activity indicated market appetite for such a financial product.

The launch also caused a scramble among other issuers such as Grayscale Investments and VanEck Associates Corporation.  They were in the process of launching their own Solana products, and the Bitwise success caused them to hurry along.  According to experts, the ETF market has been rising ever since the product was first introduced.

Why Solana

Solana offers a compelling alternative to Bitcoin.  It has a high-throughput, low-fee layer-1 blockchain with an active DeFi ecosystem and supports tokenization and NFTs.  The large, liquid futures also made it a great subject for the ETF spot, making the authorization process easier.

The second wave of crypto ETFs includes smaller altcoins after Bitcoin and Ethereum have proven profitable.  Solana is therefore the perfect candidate to lead this wave.  The additional staking feature is also a plus, since it shows how innovative cryptos can be within traditional finance.

## The Risks

There are also risk

sources.title: Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to 100% for Solana Trust (GSOL), Offering 7.23% Staking Rewards Rate

Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to

# [![Grayscale_G_Blackberry.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/4b944bce-637f-4386-ac51-330819cae5f3?size=3)](https://grayscale.com/)
# Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to 100% for Solana Trust (GSOL), Offering 7.23% Staking Rewards Rate
## Product enhancements, real-time staking rewards, and diversified validators help position GSOL as cost-efficient, transparent Solana ETP
November 05, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Grayscale Investments](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Grayscale%20Investments)FollowGrayscale Investments
Share
* * * * * * * * STAMFORD, Conn., Nov. 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Grayscale Investments®, the world’s largest digital asset-focused investment platform\*, today announced that it has waived the sponsor's fee and reduced the fees associated with staking for Grayscale Solana Trust ETF (Ticker: GSOL) for up to three months, or until the fund’s AUM reaches $1 billion, whichever occurs first. The fund is also now staking up to 100% of its SOL at a 7.23% Staking Reward Rate\*\*, and the fee waiver applies to both new and existing GSOL investors.\*\*\*
*GSOL, an exchange traded product, is not registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (the “40 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same regulations and protections as 40 Act-registered ETFs and mutual funds. An investment in GSOL involves significant risk, including possible loss of principal. GSOL holds Solana; however, an investment in GSOL is not a direct investment in Solana.*
“By waiving the management fee and reducing the staking fee for GSOL, we’re directing more of the economics to investors,” said Inkoo Kang, Senior Vice President, ETFs, at Grayscale. “We have been staking in GSOL since October 6th, even before it became an ETP. GSOL aims to deliver real long-term benefits for investors, highlighted by our diversified validator approach, a key aspect of the staking program deployed in GSOL.”
Solana is a high-performance, proof-of-stake blockchain known for high throughput and low fees, fueling adoption from new users to institutions. GSOL offers exchange-listed exposure to Solana and live staking rewards via a leading staking program, now with a temporary waiver of the sponsor's fee and reduced staking fee to further benefit shareholders.
GSOL launched in 2021 as a private placement, listed on OTCQX in 2023, and in October 2025 began staking and uplisted as an ETP shortly thereafter. Grayscale stakes up to 100% of the fund, offering investors the potential to compound over time.
For additional information about GSOL, please visit:[https://etfs.grayscale.com/gsol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=16eqWSY1y9Sa6QZhdKhmLwyoNlhn3JbMXzEQbc8obtCAHpVJJs79j-ZEvbGpUgWtEJRkGLqrSgRkx4btfW4xIW4haPMnRA9Xg7HCRjga9xl3rCY8zFCAHyR_cjgq-Tnu)
**About****Grayscale**
Grayscale enables investors to access the digital economy through a family of future-forward investment products. Founded in 2013, Grayscale has a decade-long track record and deep expertise as a digital asset-focused investment platform. Investors, advisors, and allocators turn to Grayscale for single asset, diversified, and thematic exposure. For more information, please follow @Grayscale or visit grayscale.com.
\*Largest digital asset-focused investment platform based on AUM as of 11/3/2025. For other companies in this category, AUM is considered as of most recent public disclosure.
\*\*Gross staking return of 7.23% represents the average total annualized return on st

tags.impact_reason: The launch of a Solana staking ETF by Bitwise, a major asset manager, marks a significant step in institutional adoption of Solana, providing mainstream investors access to staking rewards, with Helius playing a key role in validation.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the New York Stock Exchange facade, re-imagined with server racks and fiber optics, as the semantic anchor. This directly visualizes the core story: the 'architecting' of a traditional Wall Street financial product (ETF on the NYSE) with the underlying, complex digital infrastructure of the Solana network (Helius nodes). The fusion of classical architecture and high-tech hardware represents the partnership. For the integrated text, I chose 'BSOL'—the product's ticker symbol—displayed as a holographic stock ticker on the facade, making it a diegetic part of this new financial world.
visual_idea.description: A monumental, wide-angle shot in a 3:4 aspect ratio, capturing the facade of a building that resembles the New York Stock Exchange. However, the iconic marble columns and stone are seamlessly fused with glowing, high-tech server racks and intricate bundles of fiber optic cables. Streams of cyan and magenta light pulse through the cables, representing data flow. Above the grand entrance, a sophisticated, holographic stock ticker displays the glowing letters 'BSOL'. The background is a vast, dark, digital space, with the network infrastructure stretching into an infinite, deep-focus background, suggesting immense scale.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, ultra-photorealistic, wide-angle, low-angle shot, 3:4 aspect ratio, capturing the imposing facade of a colossal building reminiscent of the New York Stock Exchange, soaring upwards. The classic architectural grandeur of polished marble columns and stone is seamlessly fused with futuristic, glowing high-tech server racks and intricate bundles of fiber optic cables. Streams of vibrant cyan and magenta light pulse and flow through the translucent cables, vividly representing data in motion, creating a dynamic light pattern. Above the grand entrance, a sophisticated, holographic stock ticker floats ethereally, displaying the glowing letters 'BSOL' with crisp digital clarity. The building stands against a vast, dark, digital space, where the intricate network infrastructure stretches into an infinite, deep-focus background, emphasizing an immense, epic scale and height. The composition utilizes a worm's-eye view, making the structure feel colossal and dominant, with significant negative space above the building to underscore its monumental presence. Atmospheric lighting with volumetric fog enhances the glowing elements, creating a dramatic high-contrast chiaroscuro effect, with subtle rim lighting outlining the architectural details. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic, high dynamic range, intricate textures, realistic materials.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the New York Stock Exchange facade, re-imagined with server racks and fiber optics, as the semantic anchor. This directly visualizes the core story: the 'architecting' of a traditional Wall Street financial product (ETF on the NYSE) with the underlying, complex digital infrastructure of the Solana network (Helius nodes). The fusion of classical architecture and high-tech hardware represents the partnership. For the integrated text, I chose 'BSOL'—the product's ticker symbol—displayed as a holographic stock ticker on the facade, making it a diegetic part of this new financial world.
visual_idea.description: A macro, 1:1 square detail shot focusing on the point of intersection. A polished, white marble corner of a classical pillar transitions flawlessly into the intricate, humming electronics of a server rack. Dozens of glowing, magenta and cyan fiber optic threads spill out from the marble, connecting to ports on the server hardware. The lighting is dramatic, catching the cold gleam of the marble and the internal glow of the technology, highlighting the texture and fusion of the two disparate materials.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, 1:1 square detail shot, centrally focused and tightly framed, filling the entire frame with intricate textures. A polished, pristine white marble corner of a classical pillar transitions flawlessly and organically into the complex, humming electronics of a modern server rack. Dozens of bright, glowing magenta and cyan fiber optic threads spill dynamically from the marble's surface, connecting seamlessly to various ports and interfaces on the server hardware. The dramatic, high-contrast chiaroscuro lighting emphasizes the cold, pristine gleam of the polished marble and the vibrant internal glow of the advanced technology, creating deep shadows and bright highlights that accentuate the distinct textures and the surprising fusion of these two disparate materials. Subtle volumetric fog enhances the ethereal glow of the fiber optics. ultra-photorealistic, V6 realism, high dynamic range, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, realistic materials, natural color.

editorial.headline: Bitwise and Helius Architect the First Yield-Bearing Solana ETF
editorial.standfirst: The launch of BSOL marks a watershed moment for institutional crypto, integrating Helius’s node infrastructure directly into a regulated NYSE product. This partnership solves the technical and regulatory complexity of on-chain staking for Wall Street’s largest allocators.
key_takeaways: BSOL surpassed $500M AUM within just 18 days of its October 2025 launch on the NYSE.
Bitwise partnered exclusively with Helius for validator infrastructure to ensure network alignment and minimize slashing risks.
The ETF structure successfully reconciles daily NAV reporting with Solana’s epoch-based staking rewards.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The launch of the **Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL)** represents a fundamental shift in how institutional capital engages with digital assets. Unlike the passive spot Bitcoin products of the previous cycle, BSOL offers active network participation, capturing the roughly 7% staking yield inherent to the Solana protocol. For **Bitwise** CTO **Hong Kim**, the distinction is critical: staking is not merely custody; it is an active engagement with the network that requires sophisticated management to avoid market distortions. By wrapping this yield in a regulated **NYSE**-listed vehicle, Bitwise has effectively effectively sanitized the technical complexities of epochs and activation queues for traditional allocators.

To execute this strategy, **Bitwise** eschewed generic staking providers in favor of **Helius**, the ecosystem’s leading infrastructure developer. **Mert Mumtaz**, CEO of **Helius**, emphasizes that institutional staking requires more than just uptime; it demands "network alignment." As the operator of the largest number of RPC and validator nodes on Solana, **Helius** leverages its deep visibility into network performance to mitigate tail risks like front-running or outages. This partnership ensures that the massive stake weight brought by the ETF supports, rather than degrades, the underlying performance of the Solana blockchain—a crucial factor for sustaining the application layer that drives the asset's value.

The approval of BSOL also signals a major regulatory breakthrough. The **SEC** has historically hesitated on staking products due to concerns over liquidity and slashing risks. **Bitwise** successfully demonstrated that Solana’s two-day epoch structure and lack of punitive slashing for standard downtime are compatible with the daily liquidity requirements of an ETF. The firm established a rigorous daily reconciliation process to strike Net Asset Value (NAV) precisely at 4:00 PM Eastern, satisfying auditors and custodians that on-chain rewards could be accurately reflected in a traditional financial product.

Ultimately, this development is being hailed as Solana’s "IPO moment." The integration of staking into a liquid, regulated instrument paves the way for the convergence of traditional finance and DeFi. **Kim** envisions a near future where investors can leverage **BSOL** holdings for liquidity at prime brokerages like **Goldman Sachs**, mirroring the composability found in on-chain lending markets. As **Mumtaz** notes, the endgame is an "Amazon of markets"—a high-bandwidth, low-latency financial layer where tokenized assets, from stocks to uranium, trade 24/7 on a unified global ledger.
